title: Specialized Resource Navigation for Children & Families - 211info
text: Every family’s situation is different, and finding the right help can be complicated and overwhelming. That’s where connecting with 211 comes in. In addition to 211info’s everyday call, text, and email services, we also offer specialized resource navigation to support child and family health and well-being. These focused areas offer one-on-one guidance from staff who are deeply familiar with the systems, resources, and supports that matter most for children, parents, and caregivers. In partnership with multiple state agencies, our specialized resource navigation is available through three focus areas: Maternal and Child Health Child Care Referrals Foster/Resource Parent Support Every 211info call offers personalized help based on each person’s situation. What sets our specialized resource navigation apart is the depth of knowledge our staff bring to their area of expertise. They have enhanced knowledge and training allowing them deeper insights into what resources are right for each person, in addition to being able to walk callers through the details, eligibility requirements, and availability of local and statewide resources. Maternal & Child Health (MCH) In partnership with Oregon Health Authority (OHA), our Maternal & Child Health resource navigation supports pregnant and postpartum individuals, parents, caregivers, and children. Our coordinators can help callers access: Prenatal and postpartum care Breastfeeding support WIC information Children’s health and home visiting programs Reproductive and sexual health services Essentials like formula, diapers, car seats, and clothing And much more! To connect with MCH, call 211, text “health” or “salud” to 898211, or email [email protected] . MCH Coordinators, Gaby and Sara, at community events in Eugene and Umatilla . Child Care Referrals Our Child Care Team offers customized referrals for child care, preschool, parenting classes, behavioral programs, enrichment activities, and more, to support early learning and positive outcomes for children. In partnership with the Oregon Department of Early Learning and Care (DELC), our child care referral specialists help families understand and access programs like Employment Related Day Care (ERDC), Preschool Promise, Baby Promise, and Head Start. We also assist families who are paying privately, those still on the ERDC waitlist, or anyone who simply needs help finding child care that fits their needs. To connect with our Child Care Team, call 211, text “children” or “niños” to 898211, or email [email protected] . Child care referral specialists are available Monday through Friday, 7 AM to 11 PM, and Saturday/Sunday 8 AM to 8 PM. Director of Child Care, Kerry, at a community event in Cottage Grove, Ore. (2024). Foster/Resource Parent Support In partnership with Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS) Child Welfare, 211info’s Foster/Resource Parent Support is available 24/7 by calling 211. This service focuses on after-hours and weekend support when caseworkers are typically unavailable, and serves as the point of contact for: Current foster/resource parents seeking help or guidance People interested in learning how to become foster/resource parents In addition, our foster/resource parent coordinator brings decades of personal and professional foster care system experience, follows up with callers to provide one-on-one support, helps maintain our database, and participates in community events to raise awareness about fostering and resource parent support services. To connect 24/7 with Foster/Resource Parent Support, call 211, text “foster” to 898211, or email [email protected] . Foster/Resource Coordinator, Don, at a community event in Wasco County, Ore. (2025). Local Engagement and In-Person Connection In addition to helping callers, each of these specialized teams take part in community outreach and engagement efforts across Oregon. From tabling at family events to community presentations and distributing materials, our teams work to ensure families know where to turn for help, and know how to use 211info’s specialty resource navigation services. One Trusted Number for Family Support 211info is Oregon’s connection point for child and family help of all kinds. To get connected to any of our free, specialized resource navigation services, call 211. Search Popular Posts Categories Equity News
